title: SacredVote — Publicly Verifiable Civic Integrity Infrastructure
description: SacredVote engineers integrity into civic infrastructure. Verifiable by voters, transparent to the public, and secure by design. Engineered by PlausiDen Technologies.

footer.colophon: © 2026 SacredVote, an integrity-infrastructure project by PlausiDen Technologies.

sections.title: Don't trust the system — verify it.
sections.lede: SacredVote engineers integrity into civic infrastructure. Verifiable by voters, transparent to the public, and secure by design.

sections.text: SacredVote is a Voter Dashboard + public audit infrastructure for elections. Engineered by PlausiDen Technologies. Verifiable end-to-end: every voter can verify their own ballot landed in the official count; every member of the public can audit the running tally without trusting any single party.

items.value: Every voter verifies their own ballot.
items.hint: Cryptographic receipts let any voter confirm their ballot is counted as cast. No trust required — the math is the proof.
The running tally is observable in real time by anyone. Audit logs are append-only, signed, and chain-of-custody-preserving.
Ed25519 + ML-DSA dual signatures. Threshold cryptography for ballot decryption. No single party can forge, alter, or suppress a count.

sections.body: Trust is a fragile substitute for verification. The math doesn't need you to trust it — the math is the audit.

sections.text: SacredVote is one of several PlausiDen-engineered systems committed to making civic infrastructure publicly verifiable. The substrate is Rust + WASM + CSS, with formal verification on the load-bearing cryptographic primitives. The same engineering discipline that ships PlausiDen-Forge's typed-content gate ships SacredVote's ballot-verification chain.

sections.title: Open sacred.vote.
sections.lede: Verify a ballot, audit a tally, or read the engineering posts. No account required.
